local_names.hindi: मुआ ची (मूंगफली चीनी चावल की मिठाई)

aliases: मुआ ची (मूंगफली चीनी चावल की मिठाई)

hindi: मुआ ची (मूंगफली चीनी चावल की मिठाई)

region_of_origin: China (Fujian / Teochew origin); adopted as iconic Singapore hawker snack
food_subgroup: Traditional glutinous rice snack — steamed glutinous rice dough coated with ground roasted peanuts and sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Muah Chee specifically. Closest parent would be a glutinous rice snack product node; not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GBQ is the closest FoodEx2 parent for a coated glutinous rice snack; no exact code exists for Muah Chee.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Muah Chee or a sufficiently close glutinous rice snack concept.

preparation_method: rolled in ground peanut and sugar coating

serving.description: 1 serving (~120g, 6–8 pieces, standard Singapore hawker portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving of Muah Chee is approximately 120g (6–8 pieces), providing around 310 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Muah Chee — HPB Singapore Hawker Snack Nutrition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore's hawker food nutrition database entry for Muah Chee. All per-100g values are taken from this verified record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Glutinous Rice and Rice-Based Products — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~80 assigned based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous (waxy) rice products, which are composed almost entirely of amylopectin and exhibit rapid gelatinisation and digestion. Glutinous rice GI is consistently reported at 75–86 in peer-reviewed literature (Björck et al.; Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (80 × 46.0) / 100 = 36.8. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~20mg per 120g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Muah Chee is very low in sodium (~16.7mg per 100g, ~20mg per serving) and poses negligible hypertension risk; the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (~80mg K vs ~17mg Na per 100g) may mildly support blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic index (~80) and glycemic load (~36.8 per serving) driven by amylopectin-dominant glutinous rice flour and ~22g icing sugar per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Muah Chee: one standard serving (120g) delivers a glycemic load of ~36.8 — equivalent to approximately 2.3 bowls of white rice — causing a rapid and extreme postprandial glucose spike that is poorly mitigated by the modest peanut fat content (~10g per serving).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (8.3g/100g) with low saturated fat (1.2g/100g); high sugar load (18.3g/100g) raises concern for triglyceride elevation with frequent consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Muah Chee has low saturated fat and zero trans fat, which is favourable for cardiovascular health, but the high sugar content (~22g per serving) can elevate triglycerides and contribute to metabolic risk if consumed regularly; occasional consumption is acceptable for most individuals without established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Muah Chee (麻糍) is a traditional Singapore hawker snack of Hokkien and Teochew Chinese origin. It is made from freshly steamed glutinous rice flour dough that is cut into bite-sized pieces using scissors directly at the hawker cart, then tossed and rolled in a mixture of ground roasted peanuts and icing sugar. The texture is soft, chewy, and sticky — similar to Japanese mochi — with a sweet, nutty coating. Per 100g: 258 kcal, 4.2g protein, 8.3g fat (1.2g saturated), 38.3g carbohydrate (18.3g sugar), 1.0g dietary fibre, 16.7mg sodium. Per standard serving (~120g, 6–8 pieces): approximately 310 kcal, 5g protein, 10g fat, 46g carbohydrate (~22g sugar), ~20mg sodium. Glycemic index is approximately 80 (HIGH) due to the amylopectin-dominant glutinous rice flour, which gelatinises rapidly and is digested very quickly; the glycemic load per serving is ~36.8, equivalent to eating 2.3 bowls of white rice. This makes Muah Chee one of the highest-GL hawker snacks in Singapore and a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit. Sodium is very low, so hypertension risk is negligible. Cardiovascular risk is moderate due to high sugar content despite low saturated fat. Muah Chee is vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is an iconic but increasingly rare Singapore hawker pushcart snack, available at traditional hawker centres. Source: Health Promotion Board Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Health Promotion Board Singapore hawker food nutrition database, which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and verified. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous values for glutinous rice with confidence 0.9.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Muah Chee is not reported in the HPB Singapore database entry; USDA FoodData Central does not contain a direct Muah Chee entry. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Singapore source for this item. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — Muah Chee contains no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 (confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Singapore source; glutinous rice dough and peanut coating contain no meaningful amounts of these nutrients). GI of ~80 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous (waxy) rice; no direct GI measurement for Singapore Muah Chee was found in the database. GL per serving = (80 × 46.0g carbohydrate per 120g serving) / 100 = 36.8. FoodOn URI is null — no FoodOn node exists for Muah Chee; closest_parent match noted. SNOMED CT code is null — no sufficiently close concept found.
